title: Quiz: Industry Case Studies
description: Test your understanding of quantum finance case studies

questions.question: What is the most common challenge reported across quantum finance case studies?
questions.explanation: Hardware noise and limited qubit counts consistently appear as the primary challenge, limiting the scale of problems that can be practically addressed.

questions.question: What theoretical speedup does quantum amplitude estimation provide over classical Monte Carlo?
questions.explanation: Quantum amplitude estimation provides a quadratic speedup: O(1/ε) vs O(1/ε²) to achieve error ε in expectation value estimation.

questions.question: Which application domain has the most mature quantum finance implementations?
questions.explanation: Portfolio optimization has the most advanced implementations because it naturally maps to QUBO/Ising problems that can be solved with QAOA and quantum annealing.

questions.question: What is the QOALA framework primarily used for?
questions.explanation: QOALA (Quantum Optimization for Asset Allocation and Liabilities Assignment) is JPMorgan's framework for quantum-enhanced portfolio optimization.

questions.question: Industry case studies show that quantum computers currently outperform classical systems on production financial problems.
questions.explanation: False. Case studies demonstrate comparable results and proof-of-concept implementations, but quantum advantage on production-scale problems has not yet been achieved.

questions.question: What is a key success factor identified across multiple case studies?
questions.explanation: Strong partnerships with quantum hardware providers are consistently identified as critical for success, enabling co-design of algorithms and early access to capabilities.

questions.question: What encoding challenge is highlighted in portfolio optimization case studies?

options.text: Discretizing continuous weights to binary qubits

questions.explanation: A key challenge is discretizing continuous portfolio weights into binary representations suitable for qubits, which introduces quantization error.

questions.question: Which lessons are commonly cited from quantum finance case studies? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Set realistic timelines for quantum advantage

questions.explanation: Key lessons include talent investment, hybrid algorithms for near-term applications, and realistic timeline expectations. Classical fallback mechanisms are actually recommended for production reliability.
